checks.finding: Round-2 edits resolved the round-1 gaps: REQ-004/Field Definitions name Security-Sensitive:true exactly and pin Cross-Model-Waiver validity to a co-located human Approval:Approved mark naming a second distinct approver. No term where two implementers would plausibly diverge.
No direct conflict between goals, acceptance criteria, constraints, or non-goals. The fail-closed waiver logic reuses the existing WFI/Second-Approval human-only rule rather than altering it.

REQ-001 and Edge Cases state every .ps1 change must remain ASCII-only for Windows PowerShell 5.1, but no acceptance criterion (AC-001..AC-014) verifies ASCII-only encoding of the modified sdd-hook-guard.ps1; TEST-001/002/003 check decision parity and short-circuit only. A non-ASCII character could ship undetected and corrupt the guard on a Windows PS 5.1 host. Missing observable acceptance criterion for an explicitly-stated hard constraint.
All four Assumptions are established facts or tied to an explicit actionable resolution (REQ-005 OIDC check anchored to pinned SHA v1.0.165). Open Questions is None with recorded resolutions.

Governance boundaries are stated with testable fail-closed contracts (B1-B3; AC-008 fail-closed default and waiver-validity condition; AC-011 CI-to-PR; AC-013 malformed-payload). The waiver grounds safety in the already-existing guard; the enforcement-mechanism choice is implementation architecture, excluded by calibration.

Field Definitions, 14 ACs each mapped to a named test, an enumerated Edge Cases list, a resolved Assumptions section, and a closed Open Questions section. The one gap (ASCII-only coverage) requires adding a coverage item for a stated constraint, not inventing product behavior.
